title: VELLA SDK Interface Control Document v1
description: Describes the embedded-SDK interface as specified in spec/icd.md §4.1 and §4.2. The schema describes the canonical Node/JSON form (camelCase keys). The Python SDK accepts and returns equivalent snake_case keys: intent, evidence_mask, authority_scope, policy_version, proof_signing_key for the request; decision, reason_code, latency_us, proof_bundle, proof_error for the response. Both key forms describe the same interface.

IntentParams.description: Parameters supplied to the SDK's govern(...) call. See spec/icd.md §4.1.

intent.description: Action intent identifier (e.g., EXECUTE_CHANGE, ESCALATE_PRIVILEGE, DATA_EXPORT).

evidenceMask.description: Bitmask asserting which evidence classes the calling application has satisfied. See spec/icd.md §4.4.

authorityScope.description: Named scope in the compiled policy; defaults to the policy's declared defaultScope.

policyVersion.description: Must match the compiled policy's version string; if omitted, the compiled version is used.

signingKey.description: PEM-encoded private key. If provided, a signed proof bundle is included in the response.

DecisionResponse.description: The decision result returned synchronously by the SDK. See spec/icd.md §4.2.

reasonCode.description: Machine-readable reason for the decision (see spec/icd.md §5.2).

latencyUs.description: Microsecond latency of the govern(...) call.

proofBundle.description: Signed proof bundle; present when a signing key was provided and signing succeeded.

proofError.description: Diagnostic string; present when signing was requested but failed.
